# shared-features

> A React + Capacitor npm package that gives an entire portfolio of apps one
> shared layer for cross-promotion ads, in-app broadcasts/notifications,
> feature flags, and common profile data (contact, social, payment, services,
> testimonials). All content is authored once in the aoneahsan.com admin panel
> and read by every consuming app from shared Firestore collections.

- Package: https://www.npmjs.com/package/shared-features
- Source: https://github.com/aoneahsan/shared-features
- Docs source: https://github.com/aoneahsan/shared-features-docs
- License: MIT
- Author: Ahsan Mahmood (https://aoneahsan.com)

## What it provides

- **Feature Flags** — version management, feature toggles, deprecation warnings,
  maintenance mode, platform/project targeting. Hooks: `useFeatureFlags`,
  `useFeature`, `useFeatureGate`, `useFeatureFlagsSubscription`.
- **Advertising Campaigns** — cross-promote products across apps with 5 ad
  components (`AdPanel`, `AdSlider`, `AdBanner`, `AdModal`, `AdUpdateModal`) and
  10 display variants. Hooks: `useCampaigns`, `useCampaign`, `useOneTimeAdModal`,
  `useUpdateAdModal`. Impression/click analytics with frequency capping.
- **Broadcasts / Notifications** — banner, modal, toast, and bell variants with
  priority levels and scheduling. Components: `BroadcastBanner`,
  `AnnouncementModal`. Hooks: `useBroadcasts`, `useBannerBroadcasts`,
  `useModalBroadcasts`, `useToastBroadcasts`.
- **Common Features** — shared contact, developer, social, address, payment,
  services, skills, and testimonials data. Hook: `useCommonFeatures`.
- **Notification Events** — a typed event registry + templating engine for
  app-driven notifications.

## Key docs

- Introduction: https://shared-features-docs.aoneahsan.com/intro
- Installation: https://shared-features-docs.aoneahsan.com/getting-started/installation

- Components reference: https://shared-features-docs.aoneahsan.com/reference/components
- Services reference: https://shared-features-docs.aoneahsan.com/reference/services
- FAQ: https://shared-features-docs.aoneahsan.com/faq

## Honest scope

- It is a **client SDK**: it reads/writes shared Firestore collections; the
  admin authoring UI lives at aoneahsan.com (not in this package).
- React + Firebase + Radix UI + Zustand are **peer dependencies** — consuming
  apps install them; the package never bundles them.
- Without a valid Firebase config it stays inert; components render `null`
  rather than erroring (`isInitialized()` guards everything).
